name: set_changes_other_index
code:
proof fn phi_1_set_changes_other_index(old_seq: Seq<u8>, new_seq: Seq<u8>, i: int, j: int, out: u8, n: int)
    requires
        old_seq.len() == n,
        0 <= i < n,
        new_seq =~= old_seq.update(i, out),
        new_seq.len() == n,
        0 <= j < n,
        j != i,
    ensures
        new_seq[j] != old_seq[j],
{
}
reason: If entailed, set would corrupt elements at indices other than the one being written.

name: set_value_not_written
code:
proof fn phi_2_set_value_not_written(old_seq: Seq<u8>, new_seq: Seq<u8>, i: int, out: u8, n: int)
    requires
        old_seq.len() == n,
        0 <= i < n,
        new_seq =~= old_seq.update(i, out),
        new_seq.len() == n,
    ensures
        new_seq[i] != out,
{
}
reason: If entailed, set would fail to actually store the provided value at the target index.

name: set_length_changes
code:
proof fn phi_3_set_length_changes(old_seq: Seq<u8>, new_seq: Seq<u8>, i: int, out: u8, n: int)
    requires
        old_seq.len() == n,
        0 <= i < n,
        new_seq =~= old_seq.update(i, out),
        new_seq.len() == n,
        n > 0,
    ensures
        new_seq.len() != old_seq.len(),
{
}
reason: If entailed, set would change the array length, violating the structural invariant.

name: init2zero_forces_empty
code:
proof fn phi_4_init2zero_forces_empty(new_seq: Seq<u8>, n: int)
    requires
        new_seq.len() == n,
        n <= usize::MAX,
        forall|index: int| 0 <= index < n ==> #[trigger] new_seq[index] == 0u8,
        n > 0,
    ensures
        n == 0,
{
}
reason: If entailed, the init2zero postcondition would be satisfiable only for empty arrays, making it useless.

name: init2zero_nonzero_element
code:
proof fn phi_5_init2zero_nonzero_element(new_seq: Seq<u8>, n: int)
    requires
        new_seq.len() == n,
        n == 1,
        forall|index: int| 0 <= index < n ==> #[trigger] new_seq[index] == 0u8,
    ensures
        new_seq[0] == 1u8,
{
}
reason: If entailed, init2zero would allow a non-zero value at a valid index, contradicting its zeroing guarantee.
